steps.title: Identify the list
steps.content: The sentence lists three programming languages: COBOL, Java, and Clojure. Each list item includes extra descriptive information, such as a date or developer. Because some of those descriptions contain commas, simple comma separation would make the boundaries between the three list items unclear.

steps.title: Separate complex items
steps.content: A semicolon after the COBOL description separates the first item from Java. Another semicolon after the Java description separates the second item from Clojure. Choice A does not separate the complex items cleanly, Choice B mispunctuates the Java description, and Choice C uses only a comma before the final item. The answer is D.
